mūla.devanāgarī: वृष्णीनां वासुदेवो ऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनंजयः | मुनीनाम् अप्य् अहं व्यासः कवीनाम् उशना कविः
mūla.iast: vṛṣṇīnāṃ vāsudevo 'smi pāṇḍavānāṃ dhanaṃjayaḥ | munīnām apy ahaṃ vyāsaḥ kavīnām uśanā kaviḥ
mūla.chapter_position: Chapter 10 (Vibhūti-Yoga (The Yoga of Divine Manifestations)), verse 37
mūla.speaker: Krishna

senses_attested_in_panel.sense: । तथा चाग्निवेश्यशाखायाम् सव्यासो वीति तमप् वै विः सोऽधस्तात्स उत्तरतः स पश्चात्स पूर्वस्मात्स दक्षिणतः स उत्तरत इति इति

advaita.reading_summary: (reading summary extraction pending; ENABLE_READING_SUMMARIES=true to generate)
advaita.english_rendering: Among the Vṛṣṇis I am Vāsudeva — your very companion here — and among the Pāṇḍavas you yourself, Dhanañjaya, are my vibhūti (divine manifestation). Among the munis (sages who reflect on all knowable objects) I am Vyāsa, and among the kavis (far-seeing seers) I am the poet Uśanas.

viśiṣṭādvaita.reading_summary: (reading summary extraction pending; ENABLE_READING_SUMMARIES=true to generate)
viśiṣṭādvaita.english_rendering: The fact that Bhagavān took birth as Vāsudeva, son of Vasudeva, is itself a vibhūti (auspicious divine attribute) — there is no other purpose for it. Among the Pāṇḍavas I am Arjuna; among munis (those who perceive the true nature of objects through manana) I am Vyāsa; and among the kavis (the discerning wise) I am present as the supreme.

dvaita.reading_summary: (reading summary extraction pending; ENABLE_READING_SUMMARIES=true to generate)
dvaita.english_rendering: Vāsudeva means the one who covers all, inhabits all, and in whom all reside — this name encodes that Hari alone is the independent, all-pervading reality. Vyāsa means the one who is distinct from (viśiṣṭa) everything, present everywhere simultaneously, as confirmed in the Mokṣadharma and the Āgniveśya-śākhā: 'Nārāyaṇa, pervading within and without, fills everything that is seen or heard in this entire world.'

śuddhādvaita.reading_summary: (reading summary extraction pending; ENABLE_READING_SUMMARIES=true to generate)
śuddhādvaita.english_rendering: Even among the Yādavas, the Lord declares his vibhūti-pervasion: 'I am the one characterized by the dharma of Vāsudeva-hood — born into that form, I am the one to be meditated upon.' For the Pāṇḍavas, Dhanañjaya is I myself, since Nara (Arjuna) is famously the aṃśa (portion) of Nārāyaṇa. Among the munis who contemplate the meaning of the Veda I am Kṛṣṇadvaipāyana; Uśanas is Śukra.

bhakti.reading_summary: (reading summary extraction pending; ENABLE_READING_SUMMARIES=true to generate)
bhakti.english_rendering: I am the Vāsudeva who is here teaching you; you, Dhanañjaya, are my very vibhūti among the Pāṇḍavas. Among the munis, those devoted to reflecting (manana) on the meaning of the Veda, I am Veda-Vyāsa. Among the kavis, the seers of poetic vision, I am the poet named Uśanas — that is, Śukra.

advaita-bhakti.reading_summary: (reading summary extraction pending; ENABLE_READING_SUMMARIES=true to generate)
advaita-bhakti.english_rendering: It was stated earlier that even the Supreme Lord's own vibhūtis are included in this list so that He may be contemplated in those forms. Among the Vṛṣṇis, I am Vāsudeva — famous as son of Vasudeva — the very one who teaches you. Among the Pāṇḍavas, Dhanañjaya is I. Among the munis, even those of profound reflection (manana), I am Veda-Vyāsa. Among the kavis, the far-sighted discerners of subtle meaning, I am the one known as the poet Uśanas, that is Śukra.

corpus_provenance.mūla: Belvalkar critical edition (BORI 1947), via Ambuda multi-witness

post_generation_repairs.fix: verb-lemma-misidentification (broader heuristic: prefix-√root canonical for all verb-tagged tokens)

so_what_questions: Why does Kṛṣṇa list himself alongside a human student (Arjuna) as a vibhūti — does this collapse the teacher-student distinction or deepen it?
Vyāsa compiled the Mahābhārata containing this very verse: what is the significance of Kṛṣṇa claiming to be the very author of the text in which this claim appears?
All four names (Vāsudeva, Dhanañjaya, Vyāsa, Uśanas) carry distinct etymological weights — is the verse also a lesson in how names encode the divine?
Uśanas/Śukra is the guru of the asuras (demons): what does it mean for the Lord to identify with the supreme intelligence even on the 'opposing' side?
If excellence in any domain is a vibhūti of the Lord, does striving for mastery become a form of worship — or does that logic risk spiritual inflation?
Arjuna is named here as Dhanañjaya ('conqueror of wealth'): given that this conversation is about to send him into battle, what burden does being a divine vibhūti place on a human being?

All six schools agree the verse points toward the highest in each category — but they disagree on whether 'highest' means identity with Brahman, transparent servanthood, or something else. What is at stake in that disagreement for how one lives?

everyday_applications.advaita: When you encounter a person of exceptional clarity or penetrating understanding, instead of projecting envy or admiration onto them as a separate self, practice recognizing that brilliance as the same Ātman that is your own ground. The vibhūti-list is an exercise in seeing through surfaces to the single luminous awareness behind them.
everyday_applications.viśiṣṭādvaita: Your unique gifts — intelligence, strength, the capacity to help others — are not self-generated; they are Bhagavān permeating you from within as your antarātman (inner controller). Dedicate each use of those gifts explicitly back to the Lord: this is the kainkarya (loving service) that Rāmānuja's school calls the highest life.
everyday_applications.dvaita: Whatever domain you excel in, remember that your excellence is on loan from Hari and returns to nothing if he withdraws his support. Cultivating genuine humility — not performance humility — is the practical corollary of Madhva's insistence on jīva-dependence (pāratantryam). Check daily: am I operating as a servant of a greater will, or as an autonomous source?
everyday_applications.śuddhādvaita: Vallabha's reading invites you to experience your own best moments — a creative flow state, a moment of athletic precision, a burst of genuine kindness — as Kṛṣṇa's own līlā happening through you. The practice is to hold that recognition lightly and gratefully in the moment itself, not retrospectively. Joy in excellence is itself prasāda (grace freely given).
everyday_applications.bhakti: Śrīdhara's plain reading points to a simple discipline: identify the teachers in your life who are genuinely illumining you right now, and receive their instruction with the same attentiveness Arjuna brings here. The Lord speaks through actual human guides; honoring that relationship is a primary bhakti practice.
everyday_applications.advaita-bhakti: Madhusūdana's synthesis suggests a meditation practice: choose one domain where you sense a genuine calling (writing, music, service, scholarship), contemplate the Kṛṣṇa-vibhūti behind the greatest exemplars of that domain, then let that love and awe draw you toward your own deepest capacity. Bhakti and excellence are not competitors — devotional intensity fuels mastery.

primary_meaning: Among the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva, among the Pandavas I am Arjuna, among the sages I am Vyasa, and among the seers of far vision I am the poet Ushanas.
